subject: Re: mdoc file issue for DM data
body:
Hi Alex,

Correct! You don't actually have to fill in all the details, because Warp
only uses some of them. I suggest you take a look at the source code of
mdocspoofer if you're a bit familiar with python, and take a look at which
fields are being filled with dummy versus real data. From a quick look, it
seems that the important stuff is:

- ImageFile
- ZValue
- TiltAngle
- ExposureDose
- SubFramePath

Please, correct me if I'm wrong Alister :)

Cheers,
Lorenzo

On Wed, Feb 16, 2022 at 3:18 PM 'Alex Hempelmann' via Warp <
warp-em@googlegroups.com> wrote:

> Dear Alister,
> thanks for the fast reply! So basically I have to create .mdoc file
> similar to the one in the EMPIAR link you send, but have to manually
> edit/replace the values with the parameters from my tilt-series, if I
> understood that correctly. Thanks, that helps a lot!
>
> Best,
> Alex
>
> (sorry if you got this message twice)
>
> alist...@gmail.com schrieb am Mittwoch, 16. Februar 2022 um 14:55:53
> UTC+1:
>
>> Hi Alex,
>>
>> Mdocspoofer won’t work for you, it’s written very specifically to deal
>> with data coming from the thermo software ’Tomo’
>>
>> You will have to generate some spoofed mdoc files yourself for your
>> tilt-series - an example can be found at
>> https://www.ebi.ac.uk/empiar/EMPIAR-10164/
>>
>> Good luck!
>>
>> Alister
>>
>>
>> On 16 Feb 2022, at 13:52, 'Alex Hempelmann' via Warp <
>> war...@googlegroups.com> wrote:
>>
>> Dear All,
>> I am Alex and fairly new to the cryoEM world (coming from the x-ray
>> world).
>> I am trying to apply the EMPIAR tutorial steps to my data. Unfortunately,
>> we have not collected the data with SerialEM, but DigitalMicrograph, thus
>> we lack the mdoc.file.
>> For the first step using .mdoc  (stack generation) it should be fine to
>> just use newstack and get away with it.
>> Later, after tilt-series alignment, the re-implementation into Warp by
>> ''import tilt series from IMOD'' requires a .mdoc file yet again.
>>
>> Anyone has a solution how I could bypass this step without .mdoc file? :)
>>
>> I found in another thread  a nice tool called ''mdocspoofer'' for
>> creating a dummy .mdoc file from tomo5 data. Would this work with DM data
>> as well?
>>
>> I tried to install it according to the instructions, but faced some
>> difficulties:I attached the error log in a txt file here.
>>
>> Maybe something obvious is off and someone could guide me into the right
>> direction. Many thanks for any input on my issues!
>> Best,
>> Alex
>>
>> --
>> You received this message because you are subscribed to the Google Groups
>> "Warp" group.
>> To unsubscribe from this group and stop receiving emails from it, send an
>> email to warp-em+u...@googlegroups.com.
>> To view this discussion on the web visit
>> https://groups.google.com/d/msgid/warp-em/12a6e277-1cc5-4e17-99fa-f6b7b798dfbdn%40googlegroups.com
>> <https://groups.google.com/d/msgid/warp-em/12a6e277-1cc5-4e17-99fa-f6b7b798dfbdn%40googlegroups.com?utm_medium=email&utm_source=footer>
>> .
>> <mdocspoofer error output.rtf>
>>
